contentBlocks.title: Why convert BMP to PNG?
contentBlocks.html:
BMP stores images without compression, resulting in very large files. PNG uses lossless compression — it preserves every pixel while significantly reducing file size (typically 50–80% smaller than BMP).

Choose BMP-to-PNG when you need to reduce file size without any quality loss. PNG is ideal for graphics, diagrams, screenshots, and any image where pixel-perfect accuracy matters.

If you do not need lossless quality, our BMP to JPG converter will produce even smaller files.

contentBlocks.title: When should you convert BMP to PNG?

items.title: Storage savings
items.description: PNG files are much smaller than BMP while keeping identical quality. Great for archiving scans.

items.title: Web compatibility
items.description: PNG is universally supported on the web. BMP is not used online.

items.title: Image editing
items.description: PNG is the standard working format in most editors. Convert BMP first for a smoother workflow.

items.title: Documents
items.description: Insert compact PNG images into documents without the bloat of BMP.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about BMP to PNG conversion

items.question: Does converting BMP to PNG lose quality?
items.answer: No. PNG uses lossless compression, so the output is pixel-identical to the original BMP.

items.question: How much smaller will the PNG be?
items.answer: Typically 50–80% smaller than BMP, depending on image content.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
Are files uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs locally in your browser.

items.question: What is the maximum file size?
items.answer: Individual files up to 100 MB are supported.
